idea.title: Satisfying Spill
idea.hook: Drag your mop across a tiled floor to absorb spreading spills before they reach the room's edges.
idea.prototype: Top-down room with 1-3 spill sources oozing outward each turn (turn = each mop stroke completed). Drag mop along grid cells to absorb wet tiles; capacity refills at the bucket. Spills spread to neighbors when adjacent to dry floor. Win = all spills capped + floor 95% dry. Lose = any spill touches a wall edge tile. Controls: click-drag continuous path.
idea.mutation_of: From Tim's IDEAS.md — mutates the satisfying-clearing-loop pattern (Apartment Cleaner, Clean Sweep Donut) into a turn-based containment puzzle. Pressure comes from a spreading front + finite mop charge, never a real-time timer.
idea.why_novel: No shipped game uses turn-based ooze-spread containment as the loop. Apartment Cleaner is real-time dust-sweep; Clean Sweep Donut is a real-time score-clear; Ice Sweeper is push-block. This one freezes the world between strokes — the only thing pushing the wet front forward is the player's own action. That tension (every cleanup move advances the threat) is a fresh pressure type for the portfolio.
idea.non_timer_pressure: spreading spill front (turn-based ooze) + mop capacity (resource scarcity)
idea.lose_condition: Any spill tile reaches a wall edge of the room

sources.title: Golden Mechanics: Airbuds
sources.snippet: Satisfying clearing/removal loops still have market pull — visible progress, area cleanup, and simple before/after satisfaction. Build a 2-hour cleanup prototype: dirty arena, rolling/dragging cleaner, escalating debris sizes, juicy reveal of clean floor.

sources.title: Satisfying Spill (pre-approved)
sources.snippet: Drag mop to absorb spreading spills before they hit the wall. Containment over collection — every stroke advances the threat.

sources.title: REFLECT - SSR For URP
sources.snippet: Cheap screen-space VFX can sell a simple mechanic — one shader-like effect can make a small prototype feel authored. Used as styling cue for the wet/sparkle tile pulse.

synthesizer_brief: Pick the highest-engagement idea from the daily Build Today list that uses a non-timer pressure axis and ships in vanilla JS canvas in one day. Spread containment satisfies all three.

alternatives.title: Ice Quarry Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap a frozen tile to shatter it into chunks, chaining cracks to clear the quarry before your pickaxe budget runs out.
alternatives.mutation_of: Procedural ice destruction (nextbiggames signal) into a budget-tap loop.
alternatives.why_novel: Chained tile destruction with hidden fracture lines as a daily-seed puzzle.

alternatives.title: Cargo Yard Dispatch
alternatives.hook: Route incoming freight cars to the right warehouse by flipping junctions before the queue overflows the inbound track.
alternatives.mutation_of: Rail logistics signal compressed into a queue-capacity loop.
alternatives.why_novel: Non-timer pressure via track capacity instead of seconds.

alternatives.title: Overcharge Donut
alternatives.hook: Hold to charge the launcher, release inside the blinking sweet-spot — overshoot and the donut detonates instead of flying.
alternatives.mutation_of: Hold-release timing mutates the Donut Launcher drag-aim into precision pressure.
alternatives.why_novel: Sweet-spot precision is a fresh pressure form for the donut-theme portfolio.

alternatives.title: Style Mixer
alternatives.hook: Each round shows the same scene in two visual styles — pick the matching tile from a grid of 9 candidates before your guesses run out.
alternatives.mutation_of: Style-mixing from game_idea/1585 compressed into a daily-seed picker.
alternatives.why_novel: Shader-like 2D filters as the puzzle surface — pure canvas, daily leaderboard friendly.
